commands.review_summary:
The branch passes message channel/provider context into embedded payload generation, suppresses exec-like and middleware tool-error warning payloads for selected chat providers/session keys, adds Telegram-focused regression tests, and updates Telegram docs.

PR surface: Source +58, Tests +71, Docs 0. Total +129 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: the PR head returns `showWarning: false` for middleware errors before cron/session exemptions, and its own updated test expects only assistant text after a middleware exec failure. I did not execute tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs author or maintainer code changes plus contributor/maintainer real behavior proof; automation cannot complete the external proof gate for this branch.

target.title: fix(runtime): suppress raw internal tool warnings in chats
target.body:
Summary
- Fail closed for exec-like internal tool failure warnings on chat message providers, including Telegram.
- Preserve cron/session diagnostic behavior while keeping raw failed commands, stderr, stack traces, and child output off chat surfaces.
- Pass message channel/provider context into embedded payload construction so direct-message sessions are covered even when their session keys are not channel-prefixed.
- Add regression coverage for Telegram direct sessions using channel-prefixed, main-scope, and per-peer session keys.
- Document Telegram diagnostic hygiene.

Validation
- git diff --check
- Review pass on focused diff
- QA pass on focused diff
- Targeted test command could not execute in this local worktree because dependencies were not installed and the package manager was unavailable; the attempted runner failed before assertions on missing packages. The regression tests are included in this PR for CI execution.
target.author: iamvishnusankar
